headline: 'After Greenland and Iran, the Hungarian election marks a new step in the transatlantic rift'
description: The crushing electoral defeat of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, despite massive support from Washington, underscores the failure of Donald Trump's ideological campaign in Europe. The gap is widening inexorably, writes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

Paris region air pollution rose in 2025
description: As French lawmakers voted to eliminate low-emission zones, the independent organization Airparif recorded a slight decline in air quality in the Paris region between 2024 and 2025.

headline: 'Cesar Chavez was no friend to the undocumented whom ICE now persecutes'
description: After recent revelations by 'The New York Times' of rape and sexual abuse cast a shadow over the legacy of the renowned American labor leader, American academic Sarah Quesada questions the glorifying of Chavez.

headline: Elysée worker sentenced for stealing presidential porcelain
description: A former silverware keeper at France's presidential palace received a two-year sentence for stealing hundreds of thousands of euros worth of luxury items between 2023 and 2025.

headline: Virginie Despentes and over 130 writers quit publishing house after Bolloré fires CEO
description: More than 130 writers said they would be leaving the Hachette Livre group after billionaire Vincent Bolloré fired Olivier Nora, the publisher of the Grasset house.

Firing of respected French publisher demonstrates billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
description: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.

headline: The Curtis Yarvin conundrum: Should one debate with the far right?
description: The reactionary American blogger close to the Trumpian figures was invited to a symposium in Germany with prominent scholars. Some critics say the invitation signals that red lines have vanished from intellectual debate.

headline: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
description: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.
